raw_text:
Nexus Labs

Principal Full Stack Engineer

LOCATION

San Francisco, CA (Remote-
first)

TYPE

Full-time

COMPENSATION

$280,000 - $380,000 + equity

TECH STACK

Node.js

TypeScript

React

PostgreSQL

GraphQL

AWS (ECS, Lambda, RDS,
S3, CloudFormation)

GCP (Cloud Run, Cloud
SQL)

Docker & Kubernetes

Git & CI/CD (GitHub
Actions, GitLab CI)

Terraform

About Nexus Labs: Nexus Labs is a venture-backed cloud
infrastructure and platform engineering company founded in
2019. We enable enterprise customers to build, deploy, and
scale full-stack applications with confidence. Our platform
abstracts complexity across microservices, real-time data, and

AI model inference—serving 500+ companies across fintech,
healthtech, and e-commerce sectors. We're trusted by Fortune
500 companies and high-growth startups alike.

We are seeking an exceptional Principal Full Stack Engineer
to lead the technical direction of our platform's core

infrastructure and developer experience. As a Principal
Engineer, you will define architecture standards, mentor senior
engineers across teams, and drive strategic initiatives that
impact how thousands of developers build applications on our
platform. This role requires deep expertise in modern full-stack
development, proven leadership of large-scale systems, and
the ability to influence organizational technical strategy.

RESPONSIBILITIES

Define and evolve the organization-wide technical strategy
for our full-stack platform, including API design standards,
microservices architecture, and cloud infrastructure patterns

Lead architectural reviews and design decisions for high-
impact initiatives; serve as the principal technical authority
for platform-critical decisions affecting multiple teams

Design and implement complex full-stack systems using
Node.js, TypeScript, React, PostgreSQL, and GraphQL that
handle millions of daily transactions with sub-100ms latency
requirements

Mentor and guide 8-12 senior and staff engineers; conduct
technical interviews, define career development
frameworks, and foster a culture of technical excellence

Establish DevOps best practices and cloud infrastructure
standards using AWS (ECS, Lambda, RDS, S3) and GCP
services; oversee CI/CD pipeline architecture and
deployment automation

Champion performance optimization, security hardening,
and scalability improvements across the platform; lead post-
incident reviews and establish reliability benchmarks

Collaborate with Product and Executive leadership to shape
the technical roadmap; translate business objectives into
platform capabilities and engineering initiatives

Building scalable, AI-readyinfrastructure for the modern web Build and maintain relationships with key technology
partners and open-source communities; contribute to
external technical thought leadership through RFCs, design

docs, and architectural guidance

REQUIREMENTS

12+ years of professional software engineering experience,
with at least 4+ years in principal-level or staff engineer
roles defining technical strategy

Deep expertise in Node.js and TypeScript; demonstrated
ability to architect and scale production systems serving
millions of users

Strong proficiency with React and modern frontend
architecture; experience building complex, performant user
interfaces for developer tools or platforms

Expert-level knowledge of PostgreSQL and relational
database design; experience optimizing queries, indexing
strategies, and scaling databases to petabyte-scale

Proven experience designing and implementing REST
and/or GraphQL APIs; deep understanding of API design
patterns, versioning, and backward compatibility

Extensive hands-on experience with AWS (EC2, ECS,
Lambda, RDS, S3, CloudFormation) and/or GCP (Compute
Engine, Cloud Run, Cloud SQL); experience with
infrastructure-as-code and multi-region deployments

Demonstrated leadership impact: mentored multiple
staff/senior engineers; led cross-functional projects affecting
organizational strategy; influenced technical culture and
standards

BONUS POINTS

Experience with event-driven architectures, message
queues (Kafka, RabbitMQ), and real-time data processing
systems

Familiarity with observability platforms (DataDog, New
Relic, Prometheus) and building comprehensive
monitoring/alerting strategies

Background in open-source projects or significant
contributions to widely-adopted libraries/frameworks

Experience scaling engineering teams, building platform
teams, or establishing engineering best practices across
multiple orgs

BENEFITS

Competitive equity package (0.5%-2% depending on role
level) in a venture-backed Series B company

 Comprehensive health coverage: medical, dental, and
vision insurance with company-paid premiums

Generous PTO policy: unlimited PTO + 10 company

holidays + sabbatical program (3 weeks paid after 5 years)

401(k) matching: 4% employer match, immediate vesting

Professional development budget: $5,000/year for
conferences, courses, certifications, and learning materials

Home office stipend: $1,500 for equipment and $200/month

for internet/utilities

Parental leave: 16 weeks fully paid for primary caregiver, 8
weeks for non-primary caregiver

Mental health support: complimentary therapy sessions via
Headspace and on-site wellness coaching

APPLY

Submit your resume, a brief cover letter outlining your
technical leadership philosophy, and a link to your GitHub

profile or portfolio to careers@nexuslabs.io. Include specific
examples of how you've shaped technical strategy at your
current or previous organization. Optionally, share a technical
RFC or architecture decision document you've authored. We
review applications on a rolling basis.
nice_to_have: queues (Kafka, RabbitMQ), and real-time data processing
